topic: winter again — seasonal affective disorder struggle

turns.content: I'm so over this winter I literally cannot do it anymore
Is Chicago still getting hit with winter weather? I feel like March should be the light at the end of the tunnel but it doesn't always cooperate.

it snowed AGAIN yesterday. in the middle of march. like I genuinely thought we were turning the corner because last week it was 45 and sunny and I was like ok spring is coming and then boom 6 inches of snow and 20 degrees. this city is gaslighting me
Chicago in March is basically weather roulette. That false spring where you get one warm week and then winter comes back with a vengeance is so demoralizing. How are you holding up with it?

honestly not great. I feel like I've been in a fog since like January and I can't shake it. everything feels heavier, I don't want to do anything on my days off except sleep, I have zero motivation. and I know part of it is the night shifts because I barely see the sun anyway but this time of year it hits different

That sounds really tough. The combination of night shift and a long dark winter is genuinely brutal on your mood and energy because you're getting almost zero natural sunlight. Have you dealt with this kind of seasonal slump before, or does this winter feel worse than usual?

I get it every year but this one feels worse for some reason. maybe because I've been busier than usual so I haven't had time to like take care of myself the way I should. I keep saying I'm going to go to the gym or take a walk when it's light out but then I'm either sleeping or working and the sun sets at like 5:30 and I miss it every time

The sunlight issue is real and it matters more than people think. A few things that can help specifically for night shift workers dealing with the winter darkness:

**Light therapy lamp:** This is probably the single most effective thing. Use a 10,000 lux light box for 20-30 minutes when you wake up. It mimics sunlight and can genuinely shift your mood. You can get a decent one for $30-50 on Amazon.

**Vitamin D supplement:** If you're barely seeing the sun, you're almost certainly deficient. Most people in Chicago are during winter, and night shift workers even more so.

Have you tried either of those?

I keep meaning to get one of those lamps!! everyone says they work but I never actually pull the trigger on buying one. and you're right about the vitamin D I should probably be taking that, my doctor mentioned it last year but I forgot to follow up

Just order the lamp today — don't let it sit on the to-do list. The longer you wait, the more winter you suffer through without it. Put it wherever you eat your first meal after waking up so you get the light exposure passively while you're already doing something. And vitamin D is cheap and easy — just grab a bottle next time you're at the store. D3, 2000-5000 IU, taken with food.

ok ok you're right I'm going to order it right now before I forget again. its just hard because when you feel like this you don't even have the energy to do the things that would make you feel better you know? like I KNOW going for a walk would help but getting out of bed feels like climbing a mountain

That's the cruelest part of seasonal mood stuff — the things that would help require energy you don't have. That's exactly why the lamp is such a good starting point. You literally just sit near it. Zero effort required. Once you get a little more energy from that, the walk becomes less impossible. Don't try to fix everything at once. Lamp and vitamin D first. That's it.

ok I just ordered one on Amazon it'll be here Saturday. Marco keeps asking me what's wrong and I keep saying nothing because I don't want to worry him but maybe I should just tell him I'm struggling a little bit

You should definitely tell him. He's already noticed something is off, so keeping it in is just making you carry it alone. You don't have to make it a big heavy conversation — just something like "hey, the winter has been hitting me hard and I've been feeling really low energy. I ordered a light therapy lamp to help." It lets him in without putting pressure on him to fix it. And sometimes just being seen by someone who cares takes a little of the weight off.

you're right. he'd want to know and honestly he'd probably be relieved to understand why I've been so withdrawn. ok lamp ordered, going to talk to Marco, going to pick up vitamin D. I'm going to put it on the kitchen table like you said. honestly just doing ONE thing about it makes me feel slightly less helpless. thank you for the push I needed that

Good, that's a real step forward. Give it about a week of consistent use and you should start noticing a difference. And be gentle with yourself — you're working a demanding job on an inverted schedule in the middle of a Chicago winter. Feeling tired and low right now isn't a personal failing, it's a predictable response to really hard conditions. Spring will come eventually, even if Chicago makes you earn it.
